title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Unsafe Consumption, and Business Abuse · Type-System Trace: Ssrf Egress Destination
summary: A gateway parses before signature verification, accepts stale duplicates, follows arbitrary callback URLs, trusts upstream JSON, and protects expensive workflows only with request counts. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts ssrf egress destination increment in signed ingress, bounded egress, and abuse decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allowlist scheme and port, canonicalize host, resolve and revalidate every destination, block private or metadata ranges, control redirects, and enforce egress network policy.
Deduplicate stable delivery identifiers, persist acceptance atomically, return promptly, process asynchronously, and classify retryable provider responses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Ssrf Egress Destination · Theory Ssrf Egress Destination
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 8200 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to combine signed ingress freshness, replay defense, and SSRF egress policy; prove cleanup before the service deadline expires; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
